titleStmt.title: Letter from Edmund Blayney Clarke to Augustine Birrell, 23 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Edmund Blayney Clarke

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Edmund Blayney Clarke regarding his recommendation as an architect to rebuild Dublin after the damage done during Easter week 1916. He encloses one of his designs to support the letter.
Birrell served as the chief secretary of Ireland from 1907 to 1916. Birrell was a liberal politician praised for his reforms of the Irish education system. The Easter Rising caught him unawares and, occupied in London, there was little he could do. A later inquiry into the Rising held Birrell chiefly responsible for the events that occurred. Birrell offered his resignation on 1 May 1916.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/9085

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
23rd May 1916 To the Right Honble The Lord Wimbourne, K.G. etc
Viceroy of Ireland,
Viceregal Lodge, Dublin 8474 aw April chief secretary's
9085 - 29 may 1916
rec'- from Vice Regal Lodge
Office 1 Ack
2 Add to offers file 1 done HJC 3/6
2:6:16 1 despt
Vob 3/6 8474 aw Your Excellency, I hope you will pardon the liberty
of writing to Your Excellency to ask if I may
be favoured with an opportunity and the privilege
of submitting designs, plans etc for the reconstruction
of Buildings in Dublin and other parts of Ireland destroyed
or damaged during the recent disturbances.
I am an experienced architect, upon a large
number of buildings of importance in England,
India, South America etc - design planning,
construction, organisation, superintendance etc of works
I enclose copies of Testimonies together with prints of
2 of my designs and if Your Excellency should so
favour me I should consider it both an honor
and a privilege to give such further particulars Your
Excellency may desire I have the honor to remain
Your Excellency's
Obedient Servant
Ed Blaney Clarke
T!
Merus
only I atta?
when rec? - in Regy
Res 29/5
SWAN AND EDGAR Edmund B. Clarke 1912
